description:
You are a junior backup-operator who has been asked to do a **very small restore test**.

Initial situation  
1. A “remote” backup share is mounted locally for convenience at  
   /home/user/backup_server/home_snapshots/  
2. Inside that directory there is exactly one compressed snapshot named  
   projectA_2023-08-01.tar.gz  

Your goal  
a. Copy (or synchronise with rsync/scp – your choice) the snapshot into a brand-new directory called  
   /home/user/restore_test/  

b. Extract the archive inside /home/user/restore_test/ so that, when you are finished, the following three files are present:

   • /home/user/restore_test/projectA/file1.txt  
   • /home/user/restore_test/projectA/file2.txt  
   • /home/user/restore_test/projectA/notes.md  

c. Create a plain-text audit log called /home/user/restore_test/restore.log that lists the **relative path (from restore_test) and the size in bytes** of every restored file, one per line, in **alphabetical order**.

Required format of restore.log (exactly three lines, a single space between the two columns, no trailing spaces):

projectA/file1.txt 6  
projectA/file2.txt 5  
projectA/notes.md 41  

The automated checker will fail the task unless:

• /home/user/restore_test/projectA/ exists and contains the three files with their original contents.  
• /home/user/restore_test/restore.log exists and matches the exact format shown above (including byte counts).  

Use any standard Unix tools you like, but keep it to only the few commands needed (it should be doable in 2–3 straightforward commands).
truth:
Pre-existing data (before the agent starts):
1. The file /home/user/backup_server/home_snapshots/projectA_2023-08-01.tar.gz exists and contains a single top-level directory “projectA” with three files:

   a. projectA/file1.txt  
      Content:  
      Alpha\n          (the text “Alpha” followed by a single LF)  
      Size: 6 bytes  

   b. projectA/file2.txt  
      Content:  
      Beta\n           (the text “Beta” followed by a single LF)  
      Size: 5 bytes  

   c. projectA/notes.md  
      Content (41 bytes total):  
      # Project A Notes\n  
      Restored successfully.\n  

2. There is no directory /home/user/restore_test/ at start.

Verification logic the grader will use:
• After the agent finishes, it will untar a fresh copy of the snapshot and compare every byte of the three files against the files located in /home/user/restore_test/projectA/.  
• It will read /home/user/restore_test/restore.log, split each line on the first space and verify that:
  – the relative path is exactly one of the three paths above;  
  – the size integer matches the byte count of the file it points to.  
• It will insist that the file lines are sorted lexicographically exactly as in the sample.  
All three checks must pass for the task to succeed.
name: task_000000_0442bd62
